title: tACS for Patients with Post-Stroke Anomia
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from two patients with chronic post-stroke anomia who underwent an 8-week individualized transcranial alternating current stimulation (tACS) intervention. Using a single-case experimental design with alternating stimulation and non-stimulation phases, the study investigated whether personalized tACS guided by individual EEG profiles could enhance naming abilities through cortical entrainment. EEG data were collected during picture naming tasks at multiple timepoints across the intervention and follow-up periods.
methods_description: EEG recordings were acquired using Starstim-32 during picture naming tasks at seven timepoints (weeks 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, and 20). tACS was applied during alternating two-week phases with frequency and montage personalized based on baseline EEG profiles. Data were preprocessed using EEGLAB and organized in BIDS format.
